title: Buttery Crispy Spanish Ice Cream Cake
description: Egg‑free, indulgent Spanish dessert that layers a buttery, crunchy cookie crust with creamy vanilla ice cream, finished with caramelized honey‑coconut topping for an energizing treat.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 °F (175 °C). Grease an 8‑inch square baking pan.
In a large bowl, whisk together the all‑purpose flour, oat flour, sugar, and salt.
Add the melted butter, honey, and vanilla extract to the dry mix. Stir until a dough forms.
Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead gently for 2–3 minutes until smooth.
Press the dough evenly into the prepared pan, creating a flat, 1‑inch thick layer.
Bake for 10–12 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown.
Remove the crust from the oven and let it cool completely at room temperature.
Spread the vanilla ice cream evenly over the cooled crust, smoothing the top with a spatula.
Place the assembled cake in the freezer for at least 2 hours to firm the ice cream.
Just before serving, drizzle the top with extra honey and sprinkle toasted coconut flakes.
Slice into squares and serve immediately for a delightful crunch and creamy finish.
